17 May 2026 Military News

Operations

Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • UK deploys new low-cost anti-drone system in the Middle East UK MOD 17 May 2026 -- British citizens and regional partners will be better protected against drone attacks as the Royal Air Force deploys a new low-cost anti-drone weapon on operations in the Middle East.

  • Shipping Firms Scramble for Emergency Truck Routes Amid Hormuz Crisis Sputnik 17 May 2026 -- The US-Israeli war on Iran has triggered a logistics shockwave — and the Gulf is now paying the price.

  • Saudis Intercept 3 Drones Fired From Iraqi Territory, Origin Unknown RFE/RL 17 May 2026 -- The Saudi Defense Ministry said three drones were intercepted and destroyed after they entered its territory airspace from Iraqi airspace.
  • Ministry of Defense: Three Drones from Iraq Intercepted and Destroyed After Entering Airspace SPA 17 May 2026 -- Spokesperson of the Ministry of Defense Major General Turki Al-Malki stated that on the morning of Sunday, May 17, 2026, three drones were intercepted and destroyed after entering Saudi Arabia's airspace from Iraq. Major General Al-Malki affirmed that the Ministry of Defense reserves the right to respond at the appropriate time and place and will implement all necessary operational measures to counter any attempted aggression against the Kingdom's sovereignty, security, or the safety of its citizens and residents

  • Abu Dhabi Says Nuclear Plant Hit by Drone Attack RFE/RL 17 May 2026 -- A fire has broken out at the Barakah nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates (UAE), according to the Abu Dhabi Media Authority on May 17.
  • HE GCCSG Condemns in the Strongest Terms the Brutal Attack on the UAE with Three Drones Targeting the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant; Reaffirms GCC Support for UAE's Security Measures Gulf Cooperation Council 17 May 2026

  • May 17: Iran vows legal action over US-Israeli aggression, says US walking into 'strategic trap' Press TV 17 May 2026 -- Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei, on Sunday sent greetings to the family of martyred top military commander, Major General Abdolrahim Mousavi, even as Iranian officials outlined a coordinated political and legal response to the US-Israeli war of aggression.
  • May 16: Iran defines post-war strategy amid failing US pressure and rise of multipolar order Press TV 17 May 2026 -- Iranian officials on Saturday framed the country's post-war stance as a phase of strategic consolidation and deepening international realignment, underscoring the failure of US pressure and the accelerating shift in the global order.

  • Trump: 'Clock Is Ticking' For Iran RFE/RL 17 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump on May 17 urged Iran to "get moving," threatening severe consequences in a post on Truth Social.

  • US military presence brings 'nothing but insecurity' to West Asia: Qalibaf Press TV 17 May 2026 -- Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf says the joint US-Israeli aggression against Iran has exposed the reality that the American military presence in West Asia brings "nothing but insecurity" to the region.
  • Leader's advisor slams Trump's threats against Iran, warns US falling into Israel's 'trap' Press TV 17 May 2026 -- Ali Akbar Velayati, a senior advisor to the Leader of the Islamic Revolution, has warned that Washington is falling into a "strategic trap" dug out by Israel and would lose what remains of its credibility in West Asia if it wages another war on Iran.
  • Iran's Top Negotiator Says World On 'Cusp Of New Order' As Trump Reiterates Warning#entry-shared RFE/RL 17 May 2026 -- Mohammad Baqir Qalibaf, Iran's parliamentary speaker and top negotiator in talks aimed at ending the war with the United States, said on May 16 that shifting geopolitics mean that the "the world stands at the cusp of a new order."

Defense Policy / Programs

  • USARPAC Hosts Land Forces Dialogue on O'ahu Following LANPAC 2026 Army News 17 May 2026 -- The U.S. Army in the Pacific hosted senior land forces leaders from Australia, Japan, the Philippines, and the U.S. Marine Corps for a Land Forces Dialogue (LFD) on Friday, May 15, 2026, on the island of O'ahu, fittingly known in the Hawaiian language as "the gathering place."

  • Our Long-Range Capabilities Are Significantly Changing the Situation - and, More Broadly, the World's Perception of Russia's War - Address by the President President of Ukraine 17 May 2026

  • Training the future force Australia DOD 17 May 2026 -- Defence innovation rarely emerges from a single lab, team or organisation. It is created at the intersection of operational need, scientific curiosity and industrial capability. When government, academia and industry work together, innovation accelerates - transforming ideas into real-world capability.
  • ADF mission data keeping pace with threats Australia DOD 17 May 2026 -- Defence scientists have demonstrated a new way of rapidly updating mission data that will enable the Australian Defence Force (ADF) to operate more effectively in fast-moving threat environments.

  • Wellington rolls out the blue carpet for visiting Japanese naval vessel New Zealand Defence Force 17 May 2026 -- The close relationship between NZ and Japan militaries was on display as the Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force (JMSDF) Mogami-class frigate JS Kumano sailed into Wellington Harbour today, accompanied by Royal New Zealand Navy frigate HMNZS Te Mana.

  • Sweden and India broaden and deepen strategic cooperation Government Offices of Sweden 17 May 2026 -- On 17 May, Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson received Prime Minister of India Narendra Modi in Gothenburg.N
  • Strategic partnership between India and Sweden - cutting-edge collaboration with historical ties Government Offices of Sweden 17 May 2026 -- On 17 May, Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson received Prime Minister of India Narendra Modi in Gothenburg on the occasion of Sweden and India upgrading their bilateral relations to a strategic partnership. N

  • Israeli army runs out of money in wake of war on Iran: Report Press TV 17 May 2026 -- The Israeli armed forces have already spent their 2026 budget in the wake of the costly US-Israeli war of aggression against Iran, a report says.
  • Iraqi farmer killed by Israeli forces after stumbling on secret base: NYT investigation Press TV 17 May 2026 -- The New York Times has reported that an Iraqi farmer was likely killed by Israeli forces after stumbling on a secret Israeli military base in the Muslim country.
  • Massive explosion hits Israeli arms facility in Beit Shemesh Press TV 17 May 2026 -- A powerful blast shook the Beit Shemesh area west of occupied al-Quds on Saturday night, with Israeli media confirming loud explosions that sent tremors across the occupied territories and triggered panic among settlers.

  • EU-Japan: Second Security and Defence Dialogue European External Action Service (EEAS) 17 May 2026 -- On 14 May, the European Union (EU) and Japan held their second Security and Defence Dialogue in Tokyo.

  • EU-Republic of Korea: Second Security and Defence Dialogue European External Action Service (EEAS) 17 May 2026 -- On 15 May 2026, the European Union (EU) and the Republic of Korea held their second Security and Defence Dialogue in Seoul.

  • Nuclear terror threat 'has never been so high' UN News 17 May 2026 -- The widespread availability of new technology, such as militarised drones and artificial intelligence, means that the current threat of nuclear terrorism is higher than it has ever been.

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Israel advances settlement scheme for southern Lebanon despite direct negotiations Press TV 17 May 2026 -- Israel's so-called security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, has revealed schemes for the expansion of illegal settlements into the southern part of Lebanon, while renewing demands for the expulsion of native Palestinians from the besieged Gaza Strip and occupied West Bank.

  • Hezbollah says Lebanon-Israel talks at 'dead end' as Israeli strikes continue Press TV 17 May 2026 -- Israel's military has attacked eastern and southern Lebanon despite a fragile ceasefire agreement, killing and injuring civilians across the country, as Hezbollah warned that negotiations between Lebanese authorities and Israel have reached a "dead end."
  • Hezbollah says it launched 19 operations against Israeli targets in 24 hours Press TV 17 May 2026 -- Hezbollah says it has carried out at least 19 military operations against Israeli positions, troops, and equipment over the past 24 hours along Lebanon's border with the occupied territories and deeper inside the southern front.

  • Hamas slams Israeli plan to turn UNRWA compound into military bases in al-Quds Press TV 17 May 2026 -- The Palestinian resistance movement Hamas has strongly condemned the Israeli regime's approval of a plan to convert the headquarters of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees (UNRWA) in occupied al-Quds into military facilities.
  • Assassination of Hamas commander sign of Israel's deceit on truce pledges: IRGC chief Press TV 17 May 2026 -- The chief commander of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has condemned the assassination of the top commander of the Palestinian resistance movement Hamas, saying the deplorable act proved the Israeli regime is always deceitful on its ceasefire pledges.

  • ICC issues 'secret' arrest warrants for five Israeli officials: Report Press TV 17 May 2026 -- The International Criminal Court (ICC) in The Hague has issued five "secret" arrest warrants for Israeli officials, including three politicians and two military personnel.

  • Qalibaf: Assassination of al-Qassam top commander during truce proves Israel's treachery Press TV 17 May 2026 -- Iranian Parliament Speaker has strongly condemned the assassination of the Al-Qassam Brigades' top commander by Israel while a ceasefire was in effect in Gaza, saying the killing is a clear demonstration of the regime's treachery.

  • Yemen reaffirms unwavering support for Palestine, Iran and Lebanon Press TV 17 May 2026 -- The leader of Yemen's Ansarullah has offered condolences on the martyrdom of a prominent commander of the Al-Qassam Brigades, reaffirming the Yemeni armed forces's unwavering support for the Palestinian people as well as Iran and Lebanon.

    • Other Conflicts

  • 'Geographic obliteration would be mutual': Pakistan fires back at India army chief Press TV 17 May 2026 -- Pakistan has responded to "provocative" remarks by India's army chief, warning that such comments could result in an escalation of tensions between the two nuclear-armed countries.

News Reports

  • Epidemic of Ebola Disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda determined a public health emergency of international concern WHO 17 May 2026 -- Pursuant to paragraph 2 of Article 12 - Determination of a public health emergency of international concern, including a pandemic emergency of the International Health Regulations (2005) (IHR), the Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO), after having consulted the States Parties where the event is known to be currently occurring, is hereby determining that the Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda constitutes a public health emergency of international concern (PHEIC), but does not meet the criteria of pandemic emergency, as defined in the IHR.
  • WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern Global Times 17 May 2026 -- The World Health Organization (WHO) declared on Sunday that the Ebola disease caused by the Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda constitutes a public health emergency of international concern (PHEIC), but does not meet the criteria of a pandemic emergency, according to an official statement posted on the WHO website.
  • Ebola outbreak in Central Africa declared a 'Public Health Emergency of International Concern' UN News 17 May 2026 -- The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern, citing rising cases, cross-border spread and significant uncertainties about the scale of the epidemic.

  • Telephone conversation with President of the Republic of Kazakhstan Kassym-Jomart Tokayev President of Russia 17 May 2026

  • Buried 'Like Dogs': In Chechnya, Oppression Doesn't End With Death RFE/RL 17 May 2026 -- In the fall of 2022, Seda Suleimanova fled Chechnya for St. Petersburg to avoid being forced into an arranged marriage, as her older sisters were. She was forcibly returned in August 2023 -- and promptly disappeared.
  • US Lets Russian Oil Waiver Linked To Iran War Expire, Reimposing Sanctions RFE/RL 17 May 2026 -- The Trump administration has allowed a controversial waiver on sanctions targeting Russian seaborne oil to expire, reimposing restrictions that had temporarily enabled countries such as India to continue purchasing Russian crude despite Western efforts to curb Moscow's wartime revenues.

  • Lai says Taiwan will not be 'sacrificed or traded away' after Trump remarks CNA 17 May 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) said Sunday that Taiwan "will never be sacrificed or traded away," after U.S. President Donald Trump cautioned Taiwan against declaring independence from China following his summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping (習近平).
  • Taiwan not part of PRC, Lai says, after Trump warning on 'independence' CNA 17 May 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) reiterated Sunday his position that Taiwan is already independent and not part of the People's Republic of China (PRC), following remarks by U.S. President Donald Trump warning against Taiwan moving toward independence.
  • Arms sales to Taiwan not a bargaining chip with China: U.S. scholars CNA 17 May 2026 -- Two American experts on Saturday said U.S. arms sales to Taiwan should not be used as a bargaining chip, after U.S. President Donald Trump said earlier that the sales were a "good bargaining chip" in dealings with China.
  • EXPLAINER / The Legislature's motion to impeach President Lai Ching-te CNA 17 May 2026 -- Taiwan's opposition-controlled Legislative Yuan will vote Tuesday in a rare but largely symbolic motion to impeach President Lai Ching-te (賴清德).
  • Trump warning against 'Taiwan independence' deals blow to DPP, Lai Ching-te: KMT vice chaiman Global Times 17 May 2026 -- Hsiao Hsu-tsen, vice chairman of the KMT, said US President Donald Trump's remarks -warning Taiwan island against pushing for "independence" because it believes the US is backing them - is a heavy blow to the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) and Lai Ching-te, in an interview with chinanews.com.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 17 May 2026

  • Qalibaf appointed as Iran's special representative for China's affairs Press TV 17 May 2026 -- Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf has been appointed as the country's special representative for China's affairs.

  • Iraq draws a red line: No territory for aggression against Iran or neighbors Press TV 17 May 2026 -- Iraq has firmly declared it will never allow its land to be exploited as a launching pad for attacks against neighboring countries, particularly the Islamic Republic of Iran.

  • PM Netanyahu's Remarks at the Government Meeting Israel - Prime Minister 17 May 2026 -- "We eliminated Hamas's number one, the master murderer Al-Haddad. I promised we would return all of our hostages, and we returned them all. Every single architect of the massacre and the hostage-taking will be eliminated down to the last one, and we are very close to completing the mission."N

  • Prime Minister holds bilateral talks with Prime Minister of Sweden India MEA 17 May 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi held bilateral consultations with Prime Minister H.E. Mr. Ulf Kristersson in Gothenburg, Sweden. N
  • Special briefing by MEA on the visit of Prime Minister to the Netherlands (May 16, 2026) India MEA 17 May 2026
